questions.question: List three key characteristics of a cloud-native software environment.
expectedPoints: Elasticity and Scalability (auto-scaling based on demand)
Resilience and Fault Tolerance (designed for failure)

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Containerized (CaaS) and Serverless (FaaS) environment models. Discuss their units of deployment, management responsibilities, typical use cases, and billing models.
expectedPoints: Unit of Deployment: CaaS uses containers/pods; FaaS uses individual functions.
Management: CaaS user manages container & app; FaaS user manages only function code.
Use Cases: CaaS for long-running microservices; FaaS for event-driven, short-lived tasks.
Billing: CaaS per resource allocation time (e.g., vCPU-hour); FaaS per execution time (e.g., GB-second).
Statefulness: CaaS supports stateful services easily; FaaS is inherently stateless.
